author: TNN
title: Central Assistant Labour Commissioner Arrested for Bribery in Kochi | Kochi News - Times of India
text: Kochi: The vigilance and anti-corruption bureau (VACB) nabbed a central assistant labour commissioner while accepting a bribe from a city-based contractor. The arrested person was identified as Ajith Kumar from Uttar Pradesh. Sources said the accused, a central govt official, allegedly demanded a bribe from the manager of a construction firm.The complainant's firm received a sub-contract for conducting repair works at a public sector company based in Kochi. Sources said it was the office of the labour commissioner that issued entry passes to the company for the workers. The accused asked for a Rs 20,000 bribe to issue the passes, said VACB sources.Subsequently, a complaint was filed with Ernakulam VACB range SP, S Sasidharan. After receiving the complaint, vigilance set up a trap to arrest the accused red-handed.The accused was booked under relevant provisions of the Prevention of Corruption Act and produced before a court. He was remanded in judicial custody. VACB would soon file a report to the department to initiate disciplinary action against the accused. Sources said a detailed investigation would be held into the dealings of the arrested official and some documents were recovered during a VACB raid held at his office and residence.DSP NR Jayaraj made the arrest. Inspectors Vinod C and Madhu R, sub-inspectors Joshy Jacob, Sukumaran, and Unnikrishnan, assistant sub-inspector Joseph, and senior civil police officers Sunil Jijimon, Dhanesh, Bijumon, Prijith, and Vineesh were also part of the team.
